commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a local-gateway config warning for large memory-watch directories, honors disabled/default/source settings, deduplicates scan roots, caps validation scan work, and expands validation-policy tests.

PR surface: Source +161, Tests +99. Total +260 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows `sources: ["sessions"]` without enabled session memory is normalized back to memory at runtime, while the PR suppresses the warning before that normalization.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused repair can align validation source gating with runtime source normalization; maintainer review still owns the bounded scan policy.

target.title: fix(config): warn on large memory watch FD risk
target.body:
## Summary

- re-add the gateway memory-watch FD warning after #89246 reverted the earlier warning-only PR
- keep memory watching enabled by default, but warn only when the relevant memory directories contain more than 2,000 markdown files
- align warning scan roots with runtime behavior by skipping default QMD memory when `includeDefaultMemory: false`
- deduplicate scanned directories within one validation pass and cap startup scan work at 20,000 streamed entries
- add validation-policy coverage for local/remote gateway behavior, disabled watch, per-agent overrides, the large-memory threshold, and QMD default-memory exclusion

## Related

- Replaces the warning behavior reverted by #89246.
- Follow-up to #89185, but now self-contained because #89185 was reverted.
- Refs #86613, where FD pressure was reproduced with large watched memory trees.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/config/validation.ts src/config/validation.policy.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/validation.policy.test.ts`
- local scan benchmark for 20,000 temp markdown files: about 13-15 ms per bounded scan on this machine
target.author: romneyda

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
